name: Husqvarna Automower
short_description: A line of autonomous robotic lawn mowers that navigate and cut a yard on their own, returning to a charging station as needed.
long_description: First sold in 1995 as the solar-powered Automower and refined across decades, Husqvarna's Automower is one of the longest-running consumer autonomous robots, quietly mowing lawns without supervision. Early models stayed inside a buried boundary wire, while recent EPOS and AI-vision models use RTK satellite positioning and an onboard camera to follow virtual boundaries and avoid obstacles with no wires at all. As a familiar appliance that acts on its own in shared outdoor space, it is a mainstream example of physical AI that people delegate real tasks to in everyday domestic life.

images.caption: A Husqvarna Automower 430X robotic lawn mower on a lawn.
images.alt_text: A low, dark-grey wheeled Husqvarna Automower robotic lawn mower sitting on a green lawn.

sources.title: Husqvarna EPOS - Virtual boundaries. Endless possibilities.
sources.access_notes: Describes RTK satellite positioning, virtual boundaries, and AI vision navigation.

interactions.title: Draw the lawn's edges on your phone
interactions.description: Instead of burying a boundary wire, you walk the mower's RTK-positioned map in the Automower Connect app and paint virtual boundaries and temporary stay-out zones around a new flower bed or a storm-soaked patch.

interactions.title: Lift it and it screams for a PIN
interactions.description: Pick the mower up off the grass and a ticking warning starts; if the right PIN isn't entered within 30 seconds a loud alarm sounds, and on GPS models a GeoFence pings your phone if it's carried past an invisible perimeter.

interactions.title: Mow the lawn into stripes
interactions.description: Set the mower to systematic mowing and it lays the grass in neat parallel tracks or a checkerboard, alternating 90 degrees each session, instead of wandering randomly — the manicured-pitch look without a push mower.

interactions.title: Asleep from dusk to dawn for the hedgehogs
interactions.description: A Wildlife Consideration schedule keeps the mower parked overnight when nocturnal animals are out, and its blades pivot and tuck under the skid plate to limit harm in a collision.

interactions.title: It becomes a named member of the household
interactions.description: Owners routinely christen the mower — Mowzilla, Grassinator, a pet's name — and talk about it as a family member rather than an appliance, waving at it as it trundles across the yard.

interactions.title: A test subject for hedgehog crash dummies
interactions.description: Oxford and Aalborg researchers built 3D-printed hedgehog dummies and ran collision trials across robotic mowers — Husqvarna among the brands now using the findings — turning a garden appliance into the object of a wildlife-safety study.

interactions.title: A roaming sprinkler-skip zone for the gardener
interactions.description: Because stay-out zones can be drawn and erased in seconds from the app, you could fence off a freshly seeded patch in the morning and reopen it weeks later without touching the mower — grounded in its existing virtual-boundary editing.

interactions.title: Quiet enough to mow while you sleep in
interactions.description: Its low noise and self-scheduling mean it can finish the lawn before you're awake, so you wake to a freshly cut yard and never hear an engine — built on the autonomous scheduling and charging-station return it already does.
